titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Yu-Kuang Sun papers
titlestmt.author: Abi Mason and Elizabeth Peters

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 28, 2021 at 23:00

descriptivenote.p: The materials of this collection are primarily in Chinese, with some in English. Materials with English include a few hymns, with Sun's Chinese lyrics as well, correspondence written to Sun's grandchildren, and one article in Sun's biographical materials. Two of the books in the biographical materials are written in Taiwanese.

did.abstract: This collection reflects the life and works of Paul Yu-Kuang Sun, a surviving prisoner of the White Terror in Taiwan from the mid-twentieth century. This collection was put together by his daughter Kang-I Sun Chang to include his writings, audio and visual copies of a selection of Sun's sermons and lectures including one at Yale University, a large selection of Sun's correspondence in both English and Chinese, and some biographical materials including published works about Paul Sun.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Paul Yu-kuang Sun, previously known only as Yu-kuang Sun, was born in China but moved to Taiwan in the late 1940's at the height of China's civil war. He moved with his late wife, Yu-Chen, primarily to be closer to her family. At that time, Taiwan was known as the Republic of China. While there, Sun taught classical Chinese, civics, geography, and basic English grammar. In 1947, though, an event known as 228 changed the course of his life for a decade.

In 1947, 228 (February 28) resulted in nearly 40 years of Martial Law under the Kuomintang (KMT). 228 is the day of a protest that followed February 27 when the actions of two agents from a government agency the day prior infuriated a democracy-seeking territory. The protest was met with violence and marked the beginning of a dark era in Taiwan's history. The decade following 228, the White Terror, was filled with extreme violence even after protests stopped. During this time, tens of thousands of people disappeared and were imprisoned and executed. Paul Sun was one such prisoner, for a period of 10 years between 1950 and 1960. When Sun was imprisoned his daughter, Kang-I Sun Chang, was only 6 years old. The KMT remained in power until 1987.

When Sun was released from prison, he remained in Taiwan until the late 1970's when he moved to the United States, upon his retirement, at the urging of his children-- Kang-I Sun Chang, K.C. Sun, and Michael Sun. Sun did not return to China until 1979/1980, and was reunited with his siblings for the first time in nearly 30 years. After missing the Cultural Revolution in China, Sun was pleasantly surprised by the improved conditions of the general public and the improvements in education. As a teacher, he was especially excited by the encouragement for Chinese students to explore world affairs— a taboo in China's communist days.

In his retired years, Paul Sun devoted himself to biblical study, calligraphy, and even authored a book called "A Kernel of Wheat" in 1998. Devoted to Christianity and the Chinese Christian community, Sun spent many years writing Chinese lyrics for popular English hymns. He gave sermons and speeches about biblical literature in churches and at Yale. Following a recorded dialogue with his daughter, Kang-i wrote a book about her experience with an imprisoned father and Paul Sun's experiences during the period of White Terror. Paul Sun has been deceased since May 2007.
Note: Information on the White Terror period found from "Taiwan's White Terror: Remember the 228 Incident" by Thomas J. Shattuck (02/27/2017)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into four series: Writing, Lectures and Sermons, Correspondence, and Biographical Materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Paul Sun papers consist of Sun's time after imprisonment during the White Terror era in Taiwan. Except for one bible, nothing in this collection is from before 1970.
The collection is split into four series: Writings, Lectures and Sermons, Correspondence, and Biographical Materials.

The Writings include a variety of items, both academic and personal, both handwritten and printed. In this series are a few biblical charts, selections of Paul Sun's calligraphy (devotional, scriptural, and miscellaneous), including a few pieces by his sons, and copies of handwritten Classical Chinese poetry. It also includes Sun's lyrical compositions, for which Sun wrote Chinese words for English hymns. A few of his academic materials are included, beginning with his book manuscript and a print copy of "A Kernel of Wheat" (1998). One article written by Paul Sun along with two other volumes of the same journal, Peking University Journal, are in this collection (1942-1943). The last of Sun's academic pieces included are 16 essay manuscripts. Diary entries from his retirement years are present, alongside two Chinese bibles and one Concordance of Old and New Testament are the final items in the Writings.

Paul Sun's Lectures and Sermons are mostly audiocassette recordings transferred to CD format and a few DVD's, alongside a selection of Sun's handwritten sermon summaries. Audiocassette tape recordings of Gaithersberg Chinese Alliance Church bible class series on John in 1994 (1a-7b, 9 CDs). There is a DVD of Sun's bible lecture at Yale University from 2001 (this lecture is also on YouTube, and recorded on CD's; tapes 8a-9) and a DVD collection of Sun's sermons from the same year. A second set of audiocassette tape recordings are also from a bible class series Sun taught at Gaithersberg in 1995 (1a-7b, 13 CDs). Two of Paul's sermons were recorded at New Haven Chinese Alliance Church in 2001(tapes 10-11ab). The last of Paul Sun's recordings are a conversation with his daughter, Kang-I Sun Chang, about the White Terror from 2002 (tapes 12a-12b, 2 CDs).

Paul Sun's Correspondence series includes stamped envelopes, a scroll of his personally inscribed seals imprinted, and letters ranging from 1970-2004. Most of the letters are addressed to Kang-I Sun Chang with a few addressed to Sun's son K.C. and his wife Lena. Although all of the latter are written in Chinese, Sun has a set of letters written to family friend Edith Chamberlin and his granddaughter Esther Sun in English. A group of miscellaneous letters to and from church friends is included, including a few emails discussing Paul Sun. One of these emails discusses Sun's post-humous compensation for what His imprisonment during the White Terror. Another is a student writing to Kang-I in appreciation of the Chinese hymns sang with Paul Sun's lyrics.

Lastly, Paul Sun's Biographical Materials includes the memorial service materials for Sun's Late wife Yu-Chen Chen Sun (a DVD recording of Sun's speech at the memorial is included), two copies of Cosmic Light Journal which have articles discussing Sun, and four books. These books include Kang-I's memoir Journey through the White Terror, and two Taiwanese and one Chinese version of Farewell to the White Terror" also by Kang-I.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Kang-I Sun Chang, daughter of Paul Yu-Kuang Sun, 2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Yu-kuang Sun papers (RG 316), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

did.unittitle: Old Testament and New Testament Book Order
Old Testament Dating, Chinese and Western Calendar
New Testament Dating, Chinese and Western Calendar
Important Notes by Paul Yu-kuang Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kang-I Sun Chang's description of item: "Harvard Library: Important information about editions of the Chinese Bible, 1863-1909."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Calligraphy by Paul Yu-kuang Sun, including a piece dedicated to his son, K.C. Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Calligraphy by Paul Yu-kuang Sun, with pieces dedicated to his sons K.C. Sun and Michael Kuan-chi Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 Scroll; 1 Thessalonians 5:23. Calligraphy by Paul Yu-kuang Sun.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kang-I Sun Chang's description of item: "This is Paul Sun's calligraphy inscription on his daughter Kang I Sun Chang's home library (See 'Paul Yu-kuang Sun: Calligraphy, Letters and Diaries' p. 142)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kang-I Sun Chang's description of item: "This is the original calligraphy for the copy that appears on p. 282 of the book, Paul Yu-kuang Sun: Calligraphy, Letters and Diaries (2019)"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hymns include "Wherever He Leads, I'll Go" , "Himself" , "Where Jesus Is, 'Tis Thee" , and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Copies of Paul Sun's favorites are in the front, and behind are original copies of hymnal lyrics organized into the seven folders Paul Sun's compositions came in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Jesus, I Come" "Count Your Blessings" "Joy to the World"

did.unittitle:
Book Manuscript - A
Kernel of Wheat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is the final manuscript of A Kernel of Wheat, Kang-I Sun Chang notes that "the other copy that is already in the Paul Sun Collection is not the final draft of the 1998 book."

did.unittitle: A Kernel of Wheat by Paul Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This copy of the book is an early edition which has many annotations, some of which are edits to be made to a later edition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There are a few notes pointing to images or references to other items of Paul Sun's Family materials. This copy is in new condition otherwise.

did.unittitle: Paul Sun's Chinese Translation of Time Article on Einstein

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Sun Chinese translation of the Time Magazine article entitled, "The Year of Dr. Einstein Centennial fever rises over the man whose ideas reshaped the universe" published February 19, 1979, Vol. 113 Issue 8, p70. Original article in English also included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This article was written by Paul Sun. This journal was only printed 8 times due to political complications. Only the Library of Congress, Cornell University Library, Stanford University Library, and Yale have copies within the United States. "…copies of this journal have become rare items in China today. In fact I have been informed by our Chinese librarian Michael Meng that currently in the U.S. only three libraries... have holdings of this journal." -Kang-i Sun Chang

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal was only printed 8 times due to political complications. Only the Library of Congress, Cornell University Library, Stanford University Library, and Yale have copies within the United States. "…copies of this journal have become rare items in China today. In fact I have been informed by our Chinese librarian Michael Meng that currently in the U.S. only three libraries... have holdings of this journal." -Kang-i Sun Chang

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This journal was only printed 8 times due to political complications. Only the Library of Congress, Cornell University Library, Stanford University Library, and Yale have copies within the United States. "…copies of this journal have become rare items in China today. In fact I have been informed by our Chinese librarian Michael Meng that currently in the U.S. only three libraries... have holdings of this journal." -Kang-i Sun Chang

did.unittitle: Tranjon Yishi Newspaper Article by Paul Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An award winning article written by Paul Sun at 16 years old, published in Tranjon Yishi Newspaper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Sun's published article of translation from Japanese. Vol. 3, no. 3 (1957), 30-34. Published while he was still in prison in Taiwan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary Entries available: Collection from 03/01/1997-11/05/1997; Copy of Article, Diary Included ; Note by Paul Sun regarding destruction of entries from 1989-2000; 08/09/2001 ; 08/23/2003; 08/24/2003; 01/23/2004; 01/25/2004

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Sun Annotations

did.unittitle: New Testament Bible with Psalms and Proverbs

descriptivenote.p: This bible includes mostly Chinese annotations, with some English notations scattered throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a an annotated copy of the Kuoyu New Testament Bible, "Shangti", with Psalms and Proverbs. Printed in Hongkong by The Bible Societies in Hongkong & Taiwan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Sun Annotations

did.unittitle: New Testament Bible with Psalms and Proverbs

descriptivenote.p: This bible includes mostly Chinese annotations, with some English notations scattered throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an annotated copy of the Chinese Union Version, Sixth Edition. "HONG KONG BIBLE SOCIETY" publication.

descriptivenote.p: This bible includes mostly Chinese annotations, with some English notations scattered throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This bible has many annotations throughout.

Includes letter on father's day 1981

Includes letter on father's day 1981

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters addressed to: Edith Chang (b. 1986), English letter to granddaughter 02/21/2004; K.C. and Lena Sun, Son and Daughter-in-Law; Kang-I Sun Chang, Daughter
Includes: Letter on 6th anniversary of wife's death, 09/10/2003 Letter regarding different Chinese bible translations, 09/21/2003

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A special collection of 12 letters in English written to his granddaughter Esther Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 2 letters, 3 emails, and the speech given about Paul Sun by Kang-I Sun Chang at his memorial on 05/14/2007.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: The opening ceremony for an exhibition about the Sun family at Tunghai Christian University, Taiwan, was recorded and can be found on YouTube.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Calligraphy; DVD of Paul Sun's Speech; and Program containing a hymn which Paul Sun composed the Chinese lyrics for.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Communication between Edith Chamberlin, Kang-I Sun Chang, Eleanor Chang and New Jersey Senator Clifford P. Case concerning the visa application and passport status of Paul Sun.

did.unittitle: Arizona Republic Paul Sun Interview Article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arizona Republic article on Paul Sun entitled, "Chinese renews ties broken 30 years ago." Interview conducted by Susan Soerfler at the Northwest Valley Bureau. This is the typescript of the interview article. Scanned copy of original article included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These are all remembrances shared during Paul Sun's memorial service.

did.unittitle: Paul Yu-kuang Sun: Calligraphy, Letters and Diaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is a brand new copy with notes regarding images and references to materials elsewhere in the Paul Sun Family materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photos of the 'Key Members of the Sun Family" (photos 1-8), and 'Memorable Pictures (Chronological' with descriptions assigned to each photo in order (known from the number on the back of each photo). Inventory for these photos provided by Kang-I Sun Chang included.

did.unittitle: Kang-I Sun Chang and C.C. Chang Family
"Mexican Writer Octavio Paz and Yale University"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally published in the China Times Literary Supplement, translated into English by Victoria Wu. Only translated version provided.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally published in Chinese, in Today, translation by Victoria Wu. Only translated version provided.

did.unittitle: "A Surgeon's Spirit of the Humanities": An Interview of Gary Price

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Matthew Towns, only translated version provided.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Matthew Towns. Both the original and translated versions provided. Includes a photo of Kang-I Sun Chang and Richard (Dick) Brodhead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally published in Chinese, in World Journal. Translation by Adam Scharfman. Only translated edition provided.

did.unittitle: Chinese Translation of Yuan History Article

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation manuscript of Yuan history and "Translation article published posthumously in 2012" by Kang-I Sun Chang. Original author uncertain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally published in the Yale Daily News, with revision. Translation by Victoria Wu. Only typed translated version provided.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally in Chinese, translation by Victoria Wu. Only typed translated copy provided.

did.unittitle: "In Memory of Beloved Professor Yu-kuang Kao"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Victoria Sancilio. Only typed translated version provided.

did.unittitle: The Life of Jim Ziegler: "Cemetery Stories"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original article and translation by Samuel Massie provided.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Literary Conviction of Harold Bloom" as published in the Yale Daily News, photos of the original article in publication included. Also provided is the original Chinese typed copy, and the translated version by Victoria Sancilio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the lives and death of Edwin and Rachel McClellan, translation by Victoria Wu. Only translated version provided.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translation by Victoria Wu, and only the translated version is provided.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes both Kang-I's and C.C.'s Statements of Faith; Battell Chapel, University Worship, The Church of Christ In Yale University, Program December 24, 2000 with notes concerning CC's involvement; The University Church, Battell Church, Yale University Program December 5, 2010, "An Ecumenical Service of the Word on the Second Sunday of Advent", with notes concerning Kang-i and CC's involvement; and a photo: "Kang-I Sun Chang attending the Christian Literature Conference at Tainan Theological Seminary in July, 1967." (sticky note pointing to her)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personally copied by hand in by Chinese by Kang-I Sun Chang as part of the 100th anniversary celebration of the Chinese Union Bible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personally copied by hand in by Chinese by Michael Sun, Kang-I's brother, as part of the 100th anniversary celebration of the Chinese Union Bible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personally copied by hand in by Chinese by K.C. Sun, Kang-I's brother, as part of the 100th anniversary celebration of the Chinese Union Bible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from Battell Chapel Sunday Services

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly a devotional diary, but with some other personal notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This diary includes prayer.

did.unittitle: Letters from Kang-I to C.C. Chang
Letters from C.C. Chang to Kang-I
Letters from Kang-I to C.C. Chang
Letters from Kang-I to C.C. Chang
Letters from Kang-I to C.C. Chang
Letters from C.C. Chang to Kang-I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included are letters written while Kang-I Sun Chang was pregnant with her daughter Edie.

did.unittitle: Letters from Kang-I to C.C. Chang
Letters from C.C. Chang to Kang-I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included are papers concerning fellowship in Geotechnical Engineering, papers concerning Master Builder Award 1995 for the Ted Williams Tunnel in Boston, and info sheet concerning the Bangkok Metro Initial System Project discussions in December 1999.

did.unittitle: Letters from Kang-I to C.C. Chang
Letters from C.C. Chang to Kang-I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes, among the letters, a speech for a memorial service of Principal Wang Hsiu, and a copy of C.C.'s CV as of 2006.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes information pertaining to Michael Sun's career.

did.unittitle: Letters to K.C. Stephen Sun and Lena Sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials relating to the publication of Kang-I's book "Women Writers of Traditional China: An Anthology of Poetry and Criticism"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This includes letters, drawings, and calligraphy from friends such as Nell Yarbrough, Laura Hutton, Haninah Levine, Chao Ling, Chu-chih and Li-li Sheng, and includes Sun family member's replies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kang-I Sun Chang's description of the item: "In 1979, Paul Sun prepared this Chinse C.V. (in his hand-writing) for his daughter Kang-I Sun Chang, in anticipation of her trip to China."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes scanned and bound copies of Kang-I Sun Chang's thesis from Tunghai University entitled, "The Importance of Herman Melville to Chinese Students with a Comparison Between the Ideas of Melville and Prominent Chinese Thinkers."

did.unittitle: Materials Concerning David Sun Chang's Death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes materials in memorandum of Kang-I Sun and C.C. Chang's first child, David, who died as an infant at only 30 days old on October 30, 1969.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes memorabilia of Edie Sun Chang, the second child of Kang-I Sun and C.C. Chang who was born May 8, 1986. Items include the record of Edie's baptism; a letter Edie wrote to the President and response from Bill Clinton from the White House to Edie on December 24, 1996 with a copy of Fall 1996 'Inside the White House' newsletter; a letter to Mayor of New Haven concerning a missing road sign and a response post card; and calligraphy done by a famous calligrapher for Edie.

did.unittitle: Radio Interview on the White Terror
Letters From Edith Chamberlin to Sun Family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photos and sections of articles.

did.unittitle: Letters from Kang-I Sun Chang, C.C. Chang, and Eleanor Chang

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials pertaining the Edith Chamberlin's life, death, and the donation of her letters to Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is volume 364 of the Chung Yuan Christian University magazine, with a photo fo the bell tower C.C. Chang designed for the university in 1963, and an article with other photos concerning C.C.'s work from 2005 anf 2020 on pages 31-32.
